Birthweight by Sex: Binary Predictor Regression

  • Goal
  • Data and Model
  • Scatter Plot With Fitted Values, SE, and CI
  • Coefficient SE, CI, and t Value
  • t Value on a Number Line
  • Model Diagnostics
library(openintro)
library(ggplot2)
library(performance)

Goal

cat("LESSON DRAFT INPUT\n")
## LESSON DRAFT INPUT
cat("==================\n\n")
## ==================
cat("Dataset: openintro::births14\n")
## Dataset: openintro::births14
cat("Outcome: birth weight in pounds, variable name `weight`\n")
## Outcome: birth weight in pounds, variable name `weight`
cat("Predictor: baby sex, variable name `sex`\n")
## Predictor: baby sex, variable name `sex`
cat("Model: simple linear regression with one binary predictor\n\n")
## Model: simple linear regression with one binary predictor
cat("Question: Do male and female babies have different average birth weights in this sample?\n\n")
## Question: Do male and female babies have different average birth weights in this sample?

Data and Model

births <- births14
births$sex <- factor(births$sex, levels = c("female", "male"))

mod <- lm(weight ~ sex, data = births)

coef_table <- summary(mod)$coefficients
coef_ci <- confint(mod)

pred_df <- data.frame(
  sex = factor(c("female", "male"), levels = c("female", "male"))
)

pred <- predict(
  mod,
  newdata = pred_df,
  se.fit = TRUE,
  interval = "confidence",
  level = 0.95
)

fitted_df <- cbind(
  pred_df,
  as.data.frame(pred$fit),
  fit_se = pred$se.fit
)

contrast_name <- "sexmale"
estimate <- unname(coef_table[contrast_name, "Estimate"])
std_error <- unname(coef_table[contrast_name, "Std. Error"])
t_value <- unname(coef_table[contrast_name, "t value"])
p_value <- unname(coef_table[contrast_name, "Pr(>|t|)"])
df_resid <- df.residual(mod)

cat("DATA SNAPSHOT\n")
## DATA SNAPSHOT
cat("=============\n")
## =============
cat(sprintf("Number of births: %d\n", nrow(births)))
## Number of births: 1000
cat(sprintf("Female births: %d\n", sum(births$sex == "female")))
## Female births: 495
cat(sprintf("Male births: %d\n\n", sum(births$sex == "male")))
## Male births: 505

cat("GROUP MEANS\n")
## GROUP MEANS
cat("===========\n")
## ===========
print(aggregate(weight ~ sex, data = births, FUN = mean))
##      sex weight
## 1 female  7.017
## 2   male  7.375

cat("\nMODEL FORMULA\n")
## 
## MODEL FORMULA
cat("=============\n")
## =============
cat("lm(weight ~ sex, data = births)\n\n")
## lm(weight ~ sex, data = births)

cat("MODEL EQUATION\n")
## MODEL EQUATION
cat("==============\n")
## ==============
cat(sprintf("Predicted weight = %.3f + %.3f * I(sex == 'male')\n\n",
            coef(mod)[["(Intercept)"]], coef(mod)[[contrast_name]]))
## Predicted weight = 7.017 + 0.358 * I(sex == 'male')

cat("PLAIN ENGLISH\n")
## PLAIN ENGLISH
cat("=============\n")
## =============
cat(sprintf("The intercept, %.3f, is the fitted mean birthweight for female babies.\n",
            coef(mod)[["(Intercept)"]]))
## The intercept, 7.017, is the fitted mean birthweight for female babies.
cat(sprintf("The sexmale coefficient, %.3f, is the fitted male-minus-female difference.\n",
            estimate))
## The sexmale coefficient, 0.358, is the fitted male-minus-female difference.
cat(sprintf("So the fitted mean for male babies is %.3f + %.3f = %.3f pounds.\n\n",
            coef(mod)[["(Intercept)"]], estimate,
            coef(mod)[["(Intercept)"]] + estimate))
## So the fitted mean for male babies is 7.017 + 0.358 = 7.375 pounds.

Scatter Plot With Fitted Values, SE, and CI

cat("FITTED VALUES WITH SE AND 95% CI\n")
## FITTED VALUES WITH SE AND 95% CI
cat("================================\n")
## ================================
print(fitted_df)
##      sex   fit   lwr   upr  fit_se
## 1 female 7.017 6.903 7.131 0.05821
## 2   male 7.375 7.262 7.489 0.05763

cat("\nPlot notes for lesson writer:\n")
## 
## Plot notes for lesson writer:
cat("- Gray dots are individual babies.\n")
## - Gray dots are individual babies.
cat("- Red points are fitted values from the regression model.\n")
## - Red points are fitted values from the regression model.
cat("- The red line connects the two fitted values. With a binary predictor, this is the fitted line.\n")
## - The red line connects the two fitted values. With a binary predictor, this is the fitted line.
cat("- Red error bars show 95% confidence intervals for each fitted mean.\n")
## - Red error bars show 95% confidence intervals for each fitted mean.
cat("- The SE column above is the standard error of each fitted mean.\n\n")
## - The SE column above is the standard error of each fitted mean.

ggplot(births, aes(x = sex, y = weight)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0, alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_errorbar(
    data = fitted_df,
    aes(x = sex, y = fit, ymin = lwr, ymax = upr),
    width = 0.12,
    color = "red",
    linewidth = 1,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = fitted_df,
    aes(x = sex, y = fit, group = 1),
    color = "red",
    linewidth = 1,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = fitted_df,
    aes(x = sex, y = fit),
    color = "red",
    size = 3,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Birthweight by Sex",
    subtitle = "Red points are model-fitted group means; error bars are 95% CIs",
    x = "Baby sex",
    y = "Birth weight (pounds)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

Coefficient SE, CI, and t Value

coef_out <- data.frame(
  term = rownames(coef_table),
  estimate = coef_table[, "Estimate"],
  std_error = coef_table[, "Std. Error"],
  t_value = coef_table[, "t value"],
  p_value = coef_table[, "Pr(>|t|)"],
  ci_low = coef_ci[, 1],
  ci_high = coef_ci[, 2],
  row.names = NULL
)

cat("COEFFICIENT TABLE\n")
## COEFFICIENT TABLE
cat("=================\n")
## =================
print(coef_out)
##          term estimate std_error t_value   p_value ci_low ci_high
## 1 (Intercept)   7.0173   0.05821 120.552 0.000e+00 6.9030   7.131
## 2     sexmale   0.3582   0.08191   4.373 1.352e-05 0.1975   0.519

cat("\nWHAT THE SE MEANS\n")
## 
## WHAT THE SE MEANS
cat("=================\n")
## =================
cat(sprintf("Estimate for sexmale: %.3f pounds\n", estimate))
## Estimate for sexmale: 0.358 pounds
cat(sprintf("SE for sexmale: %.3f pounds\n", std_error))
## SE for sexmale: 0.082 pounds
cat("The SE is the estimated sampling variability of the male-minus-female difference.\n")
## The SE is the estimated sampling variability of the male-minus-female difference.
cat("A smaller SE would mean the estimated difference is more precise.\n\n")
## A smaller SE would mean the estimated difference is more precise.

cat("WHAT THE 95% CI MEANS\n")
## WHAT THE 95% CI MEANS
cat("=====================\n")
## =====================
cat(sprintf("95%% CI for sexmale: %.3f to %.3f pounds\n",
            coef_ci[contrast_name, 1], coef_ci[contrast_name, 2]))
## 95% CI for sexmale: 0.197 to 0.519 pounds
cat("This is the plausible range for the population male-minus-female mean difference.\n")
## This is the plausible range for the population male-minus-female mean difference.
cat("Because the interval is above 0, the fitted difference is positive and statistically distinguishable from 0 at about the 5% level.\n\n")
## Because the interval is above 0, the fitted difference is positive and statistically distinguishable from 0 at about the 5% level.

cat("WHAT THE t VALUE MEANS\n")
## WHAT THE t VALUE MEANS
cat("======================\n")
## ======================
cat("The t value asks: how many standard errors away from 0 is this estimate?\n")
## The t value asks: how many standard errors away from 0 is this estimate?
cat(sprintf("t = estimate / SE = %.3f / %.3f = %.3f\n",
            estimate, std_error, t_value))
## t = estimate / SE = 0.358 / 0.082 = 4.373
cat(sprintf("Here, the male-minus-female estimate is %.2f SEs above 0.\n", t_value))
## Here, the male-minus-female estimate is 4.37 SEs above 0.
cat(sprintf("Residual degrees of freedom: %d\n", df_resid))
## Residual degrees of freedom: 998
cat(sprintf("Two-sided p-value: %.5f\n\n", p_value))
## Two-sided p-value: 0.00001

t Value on a Number Line

x_max <- max(6, ceiling(abs(t_value) + 1))
t_grid <- data.frame(x = seq(-x_max, x_max, length.out = 1000))
t_grid$density <- dt(t_grid$x, df = df_resid)
t_grid$tail <- abs(t_grid$x) >= abs(t_value)

cat("NUMBER LINE INTERPRETATION\n")
## NUMBER LINE INTERPRETATION
cat("==========================\n")
## ==========================
cat("The null hypothesis is that the male-minus-female difference is 0 pounds.\n")
## The null hypothesis is that the male-minus-female difference is 0 pounds.
cat("On the t number line, 0 means the estimate is exactly 0 SEs away from the null.\n")
## On the t number line, 0 means the estimate is exactly 0 SEs away from the null.
cat(sprintf("Our observed t value is %.3f, so it sits far to the right of 0.\n", t_value))
## Our observed t value is 4.373, so it sits far to the right of 0.
cat("The shaded tails are values at least this far from 0 in either direction.\n")
## The shaded tails are values at least this far from 0 in either direction.
cat("Those tails correspond to the two-sided p-value.\n\n")
## Those tails correspond to the two-sided p-value.

ggplot(t_grid, aes(x = x, y = density)) +
  geom_area(
    data = subset(t_grid, tail),
    aes(x = x, y = density),
    fill = "red",
    alpha = 0.3
  ) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = t_value, color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = t_value,
    y = max(t_grid$density) * 0.85,
    label = sprintf("observed t = %.2f", t_value),
    hjust = -0.05,
    color = "red"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "The t Value on a Number Line",
    subtitle = "t = estimate divided by standard error",
    x = "t statistic: number of SEs from 0",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

Model Diagnostics

cat("MODEL DIAGNOSTICS\n")
## MODEL DIAGNOSTICS
cat("=================\n")
## =================
cat("These plots check the usual linear model assumptions: residual shape, equal variance, influential observations, and normality.\n\n")
## These plots check the usual linear model assumptions: residual shape, equal variance, influential observations, and normality.

check_model(mod)
